recommended_scope.summary: Scope a narrow implementation that prevents max_adverse_r fail-safe from preempting declared stop handling when the same market bar also satisfies the declared stop trigger.
recommended_scope.preferred_option: Option D: stop-priority correction for max-adverse fail-safe overlap
recommended_scope.option_d_clarification: This may make max_adverse_r=1.0 redundant only for max-adverse bars where the declared stop would also trigger. Other fail-safe subtypes remain meaningful and must not be removed.
non_goals: no qfa-611 verdict reinterpretation in the implementation PR

fill_model_review: fail-safe exit_price uses market.mark_price with entry_price fallback
real-archive mark_price is bar.close at bar.last_record_ts_ns
evaluatePositionManager currently evaluates fail-safe before declared stop-hit
same-bar stop+fail-safe synthetic case exits as fail_safe at 20020 while stop baseline exits at 20010
PR #277 stop_loss-to-fail_safe matched deterioration averaged -1263.56 cents / 0.7613R beyond pre-fix stop-risk proxy
live_parity_scan: paper/live runner subscribes ORDER_INTENT events and routes them through BrokerAdapterRuntimeIntegration
management actions are converted in orchestration runner through createManagementExitOrderIntent
createManagementExitOrderIntent creates a market order intent for exits
simulated execution fills market and stop-market intents against BBO with marketable adverse slippage
position-manager fail-safe journal action still carries mark_price-derived exit_price, so implementation must keep journal/backtest and order-intent parity explicit
required_preservation: hard fail-safe classes such as stale market, invalid price, missing stop, invalid quantity, invalid target-position, and profile mismatch remain guardrails
max_spread_ticks fail-safe remains a separate guardrail
declared stop handling remains the source of stop_loss fills when declared stop is touched
paper/live order intent path remains market-order based unless a separate broker semantics ticket changes it

likely_files: apps/strategy_runtime/tests/integration or orchestration tests covering management action to ORDER_INTENT parity
required_tests: same-bar declared stop and max_adverse_r should resolve as stop_loss at active_stop_price
max_adverse_r still emits fail_safe when declared stop is unavailable or not touched but adverse-R threshold is exceeded
hard fail-safe reasons still preempt normal management when data/profile/position validity is compromised
max_spread_ticks fail-safe behavior remains separately covered
paper/live management exit order intent parity is asserted for FAIL_SAFE_EXIT and stop/exit actions
no strategy roster, profile, or authority mutation

rejected_options.reason: Leaves documented FILL_MODEL_PESSIMISTIC stop-cross attribution intact.

rejected_options.option: Globally use active_stop_price for fail-safe exits
rejected_options.reason: Would be over-broad and wrong for spread, stale, invalid, and non-stop-overlap fail-safe cases.

rejected_options.option: Globally move all fail-safe checks after stop-hit
rejected_options.reason: Could weaken hard safety checks such as stale market, invalid price, invalid quantity, or profile mismatch.

rejected_options.option: Use ask/bid directly in position-manager fail-safe journals
rejected_options.reason: May be a live-fill realism topic, but current evidence points first to same-bar stop-preemption in real-archive attribution.

authority_caveat: This decision creates no engine change, strategy change, roster change, paper observation, broker/live dispatch, Phase 6 authority, or ADR reinterpretation.
